description: Configure the Chrome browser on a commercial fishing vessel's bridge terminal to operate over a high-latency, metered satellite internet connection. Organize the 23 unassorted bookmarks into five folders: 'Weather & Sea State', 'Vessel Tracking', 'Catch Reporting', 'Port Authorities', and 'Crew'. Optimize for low bandwidth by blocking images globally, with explicit allow exceptions for 'ocean.weather.gov', 'marine.rutgers.edu', and 'oscar.noaa.gov'. Enable the 'Parallel downloading' flag and disable 'Smooth Scrolling'. Create a custom search engine shortcut with the keyword 'mmsi' and the URL 'https://www.marinetraffic.com/en/ais/details/ships/mmsi:%s'. Set the browser to open 'https://ocean.weather.gov' and 'https://www.marinetraffic.com' on startup. Finally, set the default download directory to '/home/ga/Documents/Catch_Logs' and disable the prompt to ask where to save each file.
